question: A flow node nested in a multi-instance body currently has 800 running tokens. The operator chooses `Cancel instance` on that flow node in modification mode. How many tokens does a single such modification cancel?

options.text: All 800 — Operate cancels every running token on the selected flow node in one operation.
Zero — modifications on multi-instance bodies are unsupported.
500 — the per-modification cap is 500 tokens; repeat the modification on the same flow node to handle the rest.
100 — Operate processes tokens in fixed chunks of one hundred per apply.

a.text: The docs state a technical limit of 500 affected tokens per cancel or move on a single multi-instance-nested flow node.

b.text: Modifications on multi-instance bodies are supported, subject to the 500-token-per-operation cap.

c.text: Up to 500 tokens are affected per cancel / move modification; the docs recommend repeating the modification on the same flow node until all tokens are handled.

d.text: There is no documented 100-token chunking; the documented cap is 500.

explanation: For cancel or move on a flow node instance nested in a multi-instance body, the docs cap the number of affected tokens at 500 per modification in this version. The operator can repeat the modification on the same flow node until all tokens are handled, or fall back to the Orchestration Cluster API with a custom filter.
